title: Multimodal LLM Applications
concept: Multimodal capabilities enable document analysis, visual QA, and accessibility tools for visually impaired users. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Open-Source vs. Closed Models.'

exercises.prompt: What does the word 'multimodal' mean when talking about AI?

options.text: Running multiple programs at the exact same time
Understanding and combining different types of inputs like text and images
Using many computers linked together in a chain

exercises.explain: Multimodal AI bridges the gap between different formats like sight (images) and language (text).

cards.title: AI with Eyes!
cards.body: Have you ever wished you could show your homework to a computer and just ask, 'Where did I make a mistake?' With Multimodal LLMs, computers can now use both 'eyes' and 'brains' to help you solve real-world problems!

cards.title: What is Multimodal AI?
cards.body: A Multimodal LLM is an AI model that can understand and connect different types of information—like text, images, and audio—all at once. Instead of just reading text, it can 'look' at a photo, explain what is happening, and write a story about it.

cards.title: The Video Call Friend
cards.body: Imagine a regular AI as a smart friend who only listens to you on a phone call. It can hear your words, but it cannot see the world. A multimodal AI is like that same friend on a video call: they can see what you are pointing at, read the sign behind you, and talk to you about all of it at the same time!

cards.title: Real-World Impact
cards.body: An accessibility app called 'Be My Eyes' uses multimodal AI to help blind users. A user takes a photo of a food product, and the AI reads the expiration date out loud, instantly transforming visual information into helpful speech.

cards.title: Myth: Two Separate Tools?
cards.body: People think multimodal AI just runs two separate programs—one for pictures and one for text. In reality, it uses a single, unified brain that connects words and images together, allowing it to understand the relationship between what it sees and what it says.

cards.title: Text-Only vs. Multimodal
cards.body: Let's look at the difference between traditional text-only models and modern multimodal models.

left.text: Reads text prompt -> Writes text response. Cannot process photos.

right.text: Looks at photos & text -> Answers questions about the image.

exercises.prompt: Why is a multimodal LLM useful for analyzing business documents?

options.text: It can write code faster than other models.
It can read a chart image and summarize the trend in words.
It can translate text between fifty different languages.
It does not require any training data at all.

exercises.explain: Multimodal LLMs can process both text and images, allowing them to look at a chart or diagram and explain the data written inside it.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Multimodal LLMs can only accept text as input and cannot receive images.
exercises.explain: Multimodal LLMs are specially designed to accept different formats like images, audio, and text as input.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe what multimodal AI combines.
exercises.sentence: Multimodal LLMs allow users to ask questions that combine both text and [blank].
exercises.explain: Multimodal LLMs are powerful because they bridge the gap between written text and visual images.

exercises.prompt: Which of these systems is a multimodal AI tool designed for accessibility?

options.text: A program that sends automatic email reminders based on a calendar schedule
An app that describes the contents and text on a medicine bottle from a photo
A simple digital watch displaying the current time of day

exercises.explain: AI-powered apps can read medicine labels and explain dosage instructions to visually impaired users by processing the image and speaking the text.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following projects relies on a multimodal LLM to function?

options.text: An app that scans a nutrition label photo and highlights allergen warnings
An app that translates typed English text into Spanish text
A calculator that computes mathematical equations from typed numbers
A database that stores usernames and passwords securely

exercises.explain: Awesome job! You've mastered how multimodal LLMs read documents, answer visual questions, and power accessibility tools. Next up: Open-Source vs. Closed Models.
